forecast_read.reason: Best Case is not defensible 22 days from close: the buyer is still mid-evaluation (RFP Round 2 response due 17 Jul), no economic buyer is engaged, and award plus partner confirmation plus contracting cannot complete by 31 Jul. This is a date and category problem on an otherwise healthy, engaged deal, not a deal problem.
forecast_read.math: 22 days to close (31 Jul 2026 vs 9 Jul 2026). Open 238 days (created 13 Nov 2025). Shortlisted since 30 Apr 2026 (70 days in stage, per field history). Forward pace: Round 2 documents received 29 Jun 2026, response due 17 Jul, award expected in July per Bosch's own 2 Feb 2026 plan (August 2026 project start, Q4 2027 first go-live); even an on-time award leaves under two weeks for partner confirmation and contracting before 31 Jul, so the date slips even in a win.

metrics.narrative: Bosch has defined clear operational scale metrics rather than a money business case: the platform must support +50% user growth and +100% transaction growth, and must handle roughly 10k concurrent users and 11M invoices per year with GDPR, 4-eyes, SOX and audit compliance (SF MEDDPICC field). Qualitative pain is well documented (fragmented best-of-breed tools, manual bid collection, siloed spend data, 11 May 2026 kickoff), but no Bosch speaker has quantified savings, headcount reduction, or cycle-time improvement in money terms on any call read. The move is to convert the scale requirements into a quantified ROI case before the Round 2 award decision.
sources: SF activity 24 Mar 2026 (non-functional requirements questions email)

economic_buyer.narrative: Salesforce's MEDDPICC field names an Economic Buyer, so budget authority is nominally identified, but this person has not appeared as a speaker or attendee on any call read across the deal and is not among the six Bosch contact roles on the opportunity. Zycus's VP for Europe and Africa flagged direct C-level engagement as the next step on 12 May 2026 ('engage with c level once we are through the current stage of testing'); testing closed on 22 May 2026 and there is no evidence this has happened in the eight weeks since. This is the single biggest hole heading into a July award decision: identified on paper, unengaged in practice.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicit and re-confirmed in the buyer's own words: functional depth and AI innovation, user-friendliness, integration capability, and innovation potential (SF MEDDPICC field). The 22 May 2026 POC closing review graded Zycus against exactly these dimensions, naming a 'very solid foundation', an intuitive user interface and the integrated AI concept as key strengths, and integration seamlessness, complex-auction maturity, ESG transparency and BYOK/SCIM security as considerations or critical deviations. The RFP Round 2 documents received 29 Jun 2026 now carry the formal evaluation forward on those criteria.

decision_process.narrative: The process is unusually well documented: RFI submitted 3 Dec 2025, RFP invite 12 Jan 2026, a 4-5 supplier long-list confirmed 2 Feb 2026, shortlisting for the POC confirmed 27 Mar 2026, a two-week onsite POC (11-22 May 2026) with daily check-ins, a formal closing review on 22 May, and now RFP Round 2 (documents received 29 Jun 2026, due 17 Jul 2026), with July award, August 2026 project start and Q4 2027 go-live as Bosch's own stated plan. The call roster has stayed at the IT and procurement working level throughout (Sabina Nordmann as project lead, Lisa-Anne Walterspacher, Bruno Streitenberger, Andre Hoepfinger, Thomas Kirmse); no Bosch executive has joined a call, so the approval chain above the working team remains unconfirmed.

paper_process.narrative: Too early for MSA/SOW terms (Shortlisted, pre-award), but a real paper-process risk already exists: Bosch's preferred implementation partner EY cannot support the engagement (8 May 2026), Dirk Fischbach sent a partner qualification matrix to Thomas Kirmse on 22 May 2026, and Bosch is approaching the partners itself ('In parallel implementation partners will be contacted', 22 Jun 2026) with no partner confirmed yet. Thomas Kirmse framed what follows the POC as 'the contract discussions and commercial discussion' (22 May 2026), but no contracting mechanics, paper templates or signature chain have been discussed. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c or incumbent-contract forcing function was surfaced in evidence.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and specific, not generic: fragmented best-of-breed procurement and CLM tools requiring multiple integrations, manual email and Excel-based supplier bid collection, no centralized control tower for purchase requests, and non-standardized processes across more than 100 plants (SF MEDDPICC field; 11 May 2026 kickoff; the roles-and-workflow questions raised by Bosch's admin testers on 19-20 May 2026). During the POC Bosch testers surfaced dozens of specific operational gaps in their current ways of working. The pain is owned at the working-team level (Sabina Nordmann, Lisa-Anne Walterspacher) rather than yet articulated by an executive sponsor.

champion.narrative: Thomas Kirmse (Category Purchasing Software & IT Services) is the named champion candidate, and SF's own MEDDPICC field is honest about the stage: 'too early to say but... he is open for calls and additional communication.' The deal owner has been building the relationship directly (an unrecorded mobile call on 12 May 2026 and a further call scheduled for 25 Jun 2026 per the 22 Jun entry), and Kirmse personally answered the commercial next-steps question at the 22 May 2026 closing review ('we are in the contract discussions and commercial discussion that would follow after this'). But no call evidence shows him advocating internally, sharing rival intelligence, or brokering executive access; the advocacy piece is still missing and the 25 Jun call outcome is unlogged.

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in Competitors__c, in any call read, or in Next_Step_History__c. The only competitive facts on record are that Zycus was one of an original 4-5 supplier RFP long-list (2 Feb 2026) and has since been down-selected to Shortlisted, through the two-week POC, and into RFP Round 2 (29 Jun 2026). This is a genuine knowledge gap, not a hygiene one: nothing in the discovery, POC, or closing-review calls surfaced a rival name, so Zycus cannot tell which vendor it is being scored against in Round 2 or whether it is winning on merit. Asking Thomas Kirmse directly who else remains in Round 2 would close this gap fastest.

critical_signals.text: Bosch opened RFP Round 2 on 29 June with responses due 17 July, holding to its stated plan of a July award and an August project start.

Bring-your-own-key encryption and SCIM identity management are a stated Bosch mandate from the 22 May evaluation review, and the written commitment Zycus promised for the following week is still outstanding as of 9 July.
The tender began with a long-list of 4 to 5 suppliers in February and no rival has been named in any conversation since, so Round 2 pricing and positioning are running blind.
The 26 May live SAP S/4HANA integration demo resolved the API questions that had frustrated Bosch's IT evaluators, and Bosch said it would reevaluate its integration assessment on the back of it.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Shortlisted finalist in Bosch's formal S2P tender with a completed, well-reviewed two-week POC and a live Round 2 due 17 July. The win case rests on functional depth and AI strength that Bosch itself validated; the exposure is an unengaged economic buyer, an unnamed competitive field, unresolved BYOK/SCIM security mandates, and a close date the remaining process cannot meet.

win_position.text: Bosch's own 22 May 2026 POC review called the platform a 'very solid foundation' with an intuitive UI and a strong integrated AI concept, and said it 'passed the majority of the evaluated use cases at least partially.'
Zycus has survived every down-select since the 4-5 supplier long-list of 2 Feb 2026 (shortlisting 27 Mar, POC 11-22 May) and received RFP Round 2 documents on 29 Jun 2026, due 17 Jul.

The economic buyer named in the qualification fields has never joined a call, and the C-level engagement John Woodcock proposed on 12 May 2026 has not happened, so no one with budget authority has been worked directly ahead of a July award.
BYOK and SCIM were called 'critical deviations' and a mandate at the 22 May 2026 review; the written commitment promised for the following week is not evidenced as delivered as of 9 Jul 2026.

deal_momentum.text: Buyer-driven cadence is live: Bosch declared the POC concluded on 2 Jun 2026, Zycus answered the feedback questions by 8 Jun, and Round 2 documents arrived 29 Jun with a 17 Jul deadline.

No recorded buyer meeting since the 26 May 2026 integration demo; June and July movement is documents and email only, consistent with tender cadence but leaving the 25 Jun call with Thomas Kirmse unverified.

customer_commitment.text: Bosch invested a two-week onsite POC (11-22 May 2026) with daily check-ins and named testers across S2C, P2O and technical tracks, and Thomas Kirmse committed at the 22 May close to step into commercial discussions.
On 2 Jun 2026 Bosch stated it does 'not anticipate any further alignment meetings', so all remaining influence must travel through the Round 2 submission and Thomas Kirmse.

deal_risk.text: 22 days from close: a 17 Jul Round 2 deadline plus a July award leaves no room for partner confirmation and contracting by 31 Jul, so the close date will slip even in a win.
EY cannot support delivery (8 May 2026) and a replacement implementation partner is not yet confirmed, a stated Bosch dependency for its August 2026 project start.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer-voiced praise on 20 May 2026 ('I like very much your intake management with Merlin. That's quite strong... you're already a step ahead, actually. That's already quite mature.') and the 22 May 2026 review's 'very solid foundation'. Positive, but delivered inside a controlled multi-vendor evaluation with no stated preference for Zycus over the unnamed Round 2 peers.

business_case.evidence: Scale requirements are explicit (+50% user growth, +100% transaction growth, ~10k concurrent users, 11M invoices per year with GDPR/SOX controls, per the qualification fields and the 24 Mar 2026 non-functional questions), but no Bosch speaker has quantified savings or ROI in money terms on any call read.

deal_movement.summary: Since 30 Apr 2026 the deal re-rated (300k to 1.2M, Pipeline to Best Case, Shortlisted), executed and closed a two-week onsite POC with a formal buyer review, and entered RFP Round 2 on 29 Jun 2026 on Bosch's July-award plan.

items.change: Stage Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted; Amount 300,000 to 1,200,000; Forecast Pipeline to Best Case (Dirk Fischbach)
Two-week onsite POC (PoC 3) kicked off at Bosch Stuttgart, then run with daily S2C, P2O and technical check-ins through 22 May
Bosch delivered its formal POC evaluation review: strengths, considerations, and critical deviations (BYOK, SCIM, API documentation)
Implementation-partner qualification matrix sent to Thomas Kirmse; Bosch to approach the partners itself
Live SAP S/4HANA integration demo with partner Rojo; Bosch's IT lead satisfied and Bosch said it would reevaluate its integration assessment
Bosch declared the POC phase concluded: 'we do not anticipate any further alignment meetings'
Zycus response to the POC evaluation delivered ('Answered questions on feedback')
RFP Round 2 documents received; response due 17 Jul 2026

day_summary.overall: Bosch moved the tender into its final round: RFP Round 2 documents arrived with a response deadline of 17 July. It is the buyer's first concrete forward step since it declared the POC phase closed on 2 June, and it holds Bosch to its stated plan of a July award and an August project start. The round runs through Bosch's sourcing process, so the movement arrived as documents rather than a meeting.

items.name: RFP Round 2 documents received
items.summary: Bosch issued its Round 2 documents; the Zycus response is due 17 July 2026.

competitive_position.summary: Bosch has run a formal, blind multi-vendor RFP since late 2025: an initial long-list of 4-5 suppliers (2 Feb 2026) narrowed to a shortlist that put Zycus through a two-week POC (11-22 May 2026), and RFP Round 2 documents landed 29 Jun 2026 with a 17 Jul response deadline. No rival vendor has been named in any call, field, or logged entry, so the current threat is unnamed and unranked; the strongest available signals are that Zycus survived every down-select and received a strongly positive, if critical, POC review on 22 May 2026.

competitors.name: Unnamed RFP shortlist peer(s)
competitors.quote: We are in a long-list of 4-5 suppliers.
competitors.source: SF Next_Step_History__c (2 Feb 2026 entry); Round 2 confirmed live via SF Next_Step__c (29 Jun 2026)
competitors.how_we_win: Lean into the agentic/Merlin narrative Bosch itself rated 'quite mature' and the single-ERP-integration SaaS design, both called out as differentiators at the 22 May 2026 closing review that a bolt-on, acquisition-based competitor cannot easily match.

competitors.name: Unnamed RFP long-list peer(s)
competitors.how_we_win: No action needed; these peers did not survive to Shortlisted per the recorded stage progression.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Bosch's own POC review maps Zycus as strong on the core platform and AI, credible but improvable on integration, and exposed on identity and key management, complex auction scenarios and ESG transparency. The exposures are exactly where the Round 2 response and the promised written commitments must land.

best_practice_check.summary: Round 2 is live and winnable on product strength; the win risks are executive access, competitive intel, and closing the trust loop on the 22 May commitments.
flags: Access to power before award: convert Thomas Kirmse's openness into a named economic-buyer or C-level session in award week; proposed internally on 12 May 2026 and still not done.
De-blind the tender: ask Kirmse directly which vendors remain in Round 2 so pricing and differentiation in the response are positioned against the real field, not a guess.

Neutralize the 22 May 'critical deviations' inside the Round 2 submission itself: the written BYOK/SCIM commitment plus the API/Swagger pack, so known scoring weaknesses are answered on paper before evaluation.

champion_strength.summary: Thomas Kirmse is open to communication and being cultivated directly by the deal owner (mobile call 12 May 2026, a further call scheduled for 25 Jun 2026 per the 22 Jun entry), which is real progress from 'too early to say' in the qualification field. But no call evidence yet shows him advocating internally, sharing documents, or pushing back on a rival, and the 25 Jun call outcome is unlogged, so his conversion from open contact to active champion is unproven heading into award.
champion_strength.source: SF MEDDPICC__c field; SF Next_Step__c (14 Apr, 22 Jun 2026 entries); Avoma POC closing meeting 22 May 2026

expansion_context.note: No sibling Closed-Won opportunity for Robert Bosch GmbH appears in the evidence read; executive access must be earned on this deal.

scope_change.detail: On 30 Apr 2026 the amount was raised from 300k to 1.2M and the stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted (field history, Dirk Fischbach) as the engagement widened from the original end-to-end S2C tender (shortlisting email, 27 Mar 2026) to the full S2P platform scope tested in the May POC (products now span iSource through eInvoicing plus Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA and iRisk).

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is Zycus's strongest card in this evaluation: Bosch requested a dedicated agentic-AI deep-dive (21 May 2026), its P2O testers called Merlin intake 'quite strong... already a step ahead' (20 May 2026), and the 22 May review listed the integrated AI concept as a key strength, while also noting some fully automated AI features are not yet available and the AI assistant 'often lacks the context awareness within the object'. The AI story is landing; the follow-through is proving the roadmap items Bosch flagged.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Bosch is actively evaluating agentic AI as a decision criterion: AI innovation sits in its stated criteria, it booked a dedicated AI deep-dive on 21 May 2026, tested Merlin intake hands-on during the POC, and asked forward-looking questions about building custom agents and strategic demand-management AI (19 May 2026).

items.detail: Close date 31 Jul 2026 is 22 days out with the Round 2 response due 17 Jul and award expected only 'in July' per Bosch's own plan; partner confirmation and contracting cannot also complete by 31 Jul, so the date slips even in a win.

confidence_signals.summary: The engagement runs on formal tender cadence: Bosch closed the POC, took over the pacing, and issued Round 2 documents on 29 Jun 2026, which is buyer-driven process, not cooling. The caution flags are channel narrowing (Bosch declined further alignment meetings on 2 Jun) and no recorded meeting since 26 May, so influence now travels only through the submission and Thomas Kirmse.

contributions.detail: AI capability is landing as a real differentiator — Bosch's P2O testers called Merlin intake "quite strong... already a step ahead" (20 May 2026) and booked a dedicated agentic-AI deep-dive (21 May).
Advanced from 4-5 long-list to RFP Round 2 — narrowed field (2 Feb 2026) through a completed POC (11-22 May), now at Round 2 documents (29 Jun), with no rival named as ahead.
No executive engagement yet — John Woodcock's proposed C-level session (12 May) still hasn't happened eight-plus weeks later. ► Force an EB meeting before the Round 2 response is submitted.

Champion not yet advocating — Thomas Kirmse is "open to communication" but MEDDPICC still marks champion as only partial, with no confirmed internal advocacy. ► Get Kirmse on record as an internal sponsor.

Implementation partner gap open — EY can't support delivery (8 May) and no replacement is confirmed even as Bosch approaches partners itself (22 Jun). ► Present a validated partner shortlist before Round 2 closes.
Known gaps are being worked, not ignored — BYOK/SCIM flagged as critical deviations (22 May review) sit alongside an active security questionnaire process (2 Apr).
RFP Round 2 is live — Bosch issued Round 2 documents 29 Jun 2026 with a 17 Jul response deadline, keeping the process actively moving.
Deep POC engagement through May — 8+ real transcript-backed sessions (Technical Deep Dive 26 May, AI Deep-dive 21 May, POC check-ins 12-22 May) show sustained two-way buyer engagement.
44 days since a real meeting — last logged session was the 26 May Technical Deep Dive; nothing in the last 30 days. ► Get a confirmed session booked before the 17 Jul deadline.

25 Jun Kirmse call unconfirmed — the 22 Jun next-step named a planned call with Thomas Kirmse for 25.6, but no completed task or meeting record shows it happened. ► Log the outcome or reschedule immediately.
No future meeting on the calendar — next_scheduled is null despite an award decision expected within weeks. ► Lock a specific date with Bosch procurement now.
Deliverable landed on schedule — Round 2 documents arriving as planned holds the deal in structured-evaluation mode rather than stalling.
Bosch ran real testing effort itself — hands-on POC with ~17 users (12 May) plus its own internal review scoring the platform (22 May).
Buyer is driving the paper process — Round 2 RFP documents formally issued 29 Jun with a firm 17 Jul deadline.
Buyer sourcing partners independently — Bosch is approaching implementation partners itself (22 Jun) after EY dropped out, showing real investment in solving the delivery gap.
No exec sponsor has stepped up — despite a request over eight weeks ago, no Bosch executive or budget owner has joined a single call. ► Ask Kirmse to broker EB access before Round 2 submission.
Paper/commercial process not started — MEDDPICC marks paper_process as a gap with no contract terms movement logged. ► Open commercial discussion in parallel with Round 2.

Unbuffered close timeline — close date 31 Jul is only 22 days out, the Round 2 response isn't due until 17 Jul, and award is only expected "in July" per Bosch's own plan. ► Validate or push the close date to match the real award timeline.
No economic buyer engaged — the proposed C-level session (12 May) hasn't happened in 8+ weeks, leaving budget sign-off unverified.
Delivery-partner risk unresolved — EY can't support (8 May) and no confirmed replacement exists while Bosch self-sources partners (22 Jun).
Buyer-flagged product gaps — BYOK/SCIM identity management called "critical deviations" (22 May) and complex sourcing/auction features rated incomplete.
Real but unnamed competitive field — a formal blind multi-vendor RFP with an active shortlist peer means Zycus isn't sole-sourced despite POC progress.
No rival confirmed ahead — despite the competitive process, no call or field entry names a competitor as leading, keeping current risk contained rather than acute.

deal_momentum.text: RFP Round 2 is live — Bosch issued Round 2 documents 29 Jun 2026 with a 17 Jul response deadline, keeping the process actively moving.
Deep POC engagement through May — 8+ real transcript-backed sessions (Technical Deep Dive 26 May, AI Deep-dive 21 May, POC check-ins 12-22 May) show sustained two-way buyer engagement.
44 days since a real meeting — last logged session was the 26 May Technical Deep Dive; nothing in the last 30 days. ► Get a confirmed session booked before the 17 Jul deadline.

25 Jun Kirmse call unconfirmed — the 22 Jun next-step named a planned call with Thomas Kirmse for 25.6, but no completed task or meeting record shows it happened. ► Log the outcome or reschedule immediately.
No future meeting on the calendar — next_scheduled is null despite an award decision expected within weeks. ► Lock a specific date with Bosch procurement now.
Deliverable landed on schedule — Round 2 documents arriving as planned holds the deal in structured-evaluation mode rather than stalling.

win_position.text: AI capability is landing as a real differentiator — Bosch's P2O testers called Merlin intake "quite strong... already a step ahead" (20 May 2026) and booked a dedicated agentic-AI deep-dive (21 May).
Advanced from 4-5 long-list to RFP Round 2 — narrowed field (2 Feb 2026) through a completed POC (11-22 May), now at Round 2 documents (29 Jun), with no rival named as ahead.
No executive engagement yet — John Woodcock's proposed C-level session (12 May) still hasn't happened eight-plus weeks later. ► Force an EB meeting before the Round 2 response is submitted.

Champion not yet advocating — Thomas Kirmse is "open to communication" but MEDDPICC still marks champion as only partial, with no confirmed internal advocacy. ► Get Kirmse on record as an internal sponsor.

Implementation partner gap open — EY can't support delivery (8 May) and no replacement is confirmed even as Bosch approaches partners itself (22 Jun). ► Present a validated partner shortlist before Round 2 closes.
Known gaps are being worked, not ignored — BYOK/SCIM flagged as critical deviations (22 May review) sit alongside an active security questionnaire process (2 Apr).

customer_commitment.text: Bosch ran real testing effort itself — hands-on POC with ~17 users (12 May) plus its own internal review scoring the platform (22 May).
Buyer is driving the paper process — Round 2 RFP documents formally issued 29 Jun with a firm 17 Jul deadline.
Buyer sourcing partners independently — Bosch is approaching implementation partners itself (22 Jun) after EY dropped out, showing real investment in solving the delivery gap.
No exec sponsor has stepped up — despite a request over eight weeks ago, no Bosch executive or budget owner has joined a single call. ► Ask Kirmse to broker EB access before Round 2 submission.
Paper/commercial process not started — MEDDPICC marks paper_process as a gap with no contract terms movement logged. ► Open commercial discussion in parallel with Round 2.

deal_risk.text: Unbuffered close timeline — close date 31 Jul is only 22 days out, the Round 2 response isn't due until 17 Jul, and award is only expected "in July" per Bosch's own plan. ► Validate or push the close date to match the real award timeline.
No economic buyer engaged — the proposed C-level session (12 May) hasn't happened in 8+ weeks, leaving budget sign-off unverified.
Delivery-partner risk unresolved — EY can't support (8 May) and no confirmed replacement exists while Bosch self-sources partners (22 Jun).
Buyer-flagged product gaps — BYOK/SCIM identity management called "critical deviations" (22 May) and complex sourcing/auction features rated incomplete.
Real but unnamed competitive field — a formal blind multi-vendor RFP with an active shortlist peer means Zycus isn't sole-sourced despite POC progress.
No rival confirmed ahead — despite the competitive process, no call or field entry names a competitor as leading, keeping current risk contained rather than acute.

cro_panel.header: Robert Bosch GmbH — Shortlisted · $1.2M · closes 31 Jun
cro_panel.intro: Shortlisted finalist in Bosch's formal S2P tender with a completed, well-reviewed two-week POC and a live Round 2 due 17 July.; The win case rests on functional depth and AI strength that Bosch itself validated; the exposure is an unengaged economic buyer, an unnamed competitive field, unresolved BYOK/SCIM security mandates, and a close date the remaining process cannot meet

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Bosch has run a formal, blind multi-vendor RFP since late 2025: an initial long-list of 4-5 suppliers (2 Feb 2026) narrowed to a shortlist that put Zycus through a two-week POC (11-22 May 2026), and RFP Round 2 documents landed 29 Jun 2026…

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Submit the RFP Round 2 response on Bosch's sourcing platform before the 17 July deadline. (by 16 Jun)
Attach the written BYOK/SCIM commitment and the Swagger/API documentation pack to the Round 2 submission. (by 15 Jun)
Have Thomas Kirmse broker an economic-buyer meeting for award week, per the 12 May plan. (by 22 Jun)
